title: rosa coriifolia var. incana
intro: Rosa coriifolia var. incana, also known as the hairy rose or Indian rose, is a species of rose plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. The hairy rose is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rosa coriifolia var. incana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support skin health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
